opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter on pullbacks into 400-430 that hold above 385 (gap midpoint); add on any retest of 405-415 with volume confirmation on the daily chart.
opportunities.rationale: Micron gapped +14% on Mar 18 earnings (prev close ~370 to 441+ open), confirming a massive Q2 FY2026 beat driven by HBM AI demand. Retraced from 461 peak to 422.90 (Mar 20 close, -4.81% on the day), still holding above gap base at 370. Susquehanna PT $525 implies 24% upside. DOWNGRADED from HIGH to MODERATE: the Mar 20 -4.81% single-day drop on elevated volume is a yellow flag — broad market selloff dragging on the name, and high-vol regime caps conviction. R:R from midpoint 415 is 1:2.0 to 475 stop 385. Invalidated below 385 (gap fill below 50% level).

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter Mon Mar 23 open in the 180-188 zone; EXIT by close Tue Mar 24 before the Mar 25 BMO print. Short holding window of 1-2 days — size accordingly. Do NOT hold through earnings.
opportunities.rationale: Cintas reports Q3 FY2026 on Mar 25 BMO (confirmed). Beat 3/4 last quarters with steady uniform rental growth. Business services compounder with 90%+ retention rates — defensive quality in high-vol regime. MODERATE: (1) 1-2 day pre-earnings window limits run-up potential, (2) one miss in last 4 quarters reduces beat confidence. R:R from midpoint 184: stop 173 (-6.0%), target 205 (+11.4%), R:R 1:1.9. EXIT before Mar 25 BMO print. Invalidated below 173.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter Mon Mar 23 in the 145-151 zone; EXIT by close Tue Mar 24 before the Mar 25 BMO print. Paychex is a defensive compounder that typically rallies into prints during risk-off environments.
opportunities.rationale: Paychex reports Q3 FY2026 on Mar 25 BMO (confirmed, 9:30 AM ET call). Beat 4/4 last quarters with consistent mid-single-digit revenue growth and expanding margins. Defensive payroll/HR services business with sticky enterprise contracts — recession-resistant revenue model. High-vol regime favors defensive compounders. R:R from midpoint 148: stop 139 (-6.1%), target 165 (+11.5%), R:R 1:1.9. EXIT before Mar 25 BMO print. Invalidated below 139.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter Mon Mar 23 in the 22.50-24.50 zone; EXIT by close Tue Mar 24 before the Mar 25 BMO print. CHWY reports Q4 FY2026 before the open on March 25. Pet spending is recession-resistant.
opportunities.rationale: Chewy reports Q4 FY2026 on Mar 25 BMO (confirmed, 8:00 AM ET). Stock is at $23.37, down -33.9% since Feb 24 earnings despite a +3.1% post-print day, creating a deeply oversold entry. Analysts expect EPS $0.28 and revenue $3.26B. Autoship subscription revenue growing 15%+ YoY; pet spending is defensive in downturns. William Blair analyst expects top and bottom-line beats. MODERATE: price near lower end of post-earnings range ($23.06-$37.01), suggesting risk of further weakness. R:R from midpoint 23.50: stop 21.00 (-10.6%), target 28.50 (+21.3%), R:R 1:2.0. EXIT before Mar 25 BMO print. Invalidated below 21.00.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter Mon-Tue Mar 23-24 in the 23.50-25.50 zone; EXIT by close Wed Mar 26 before the Mar 27 BMO print. CORRECTED: CCL reports Q1 FY2026 on Mar 27 BMO (not Mar 25 AMC as previously stated — confirmed via stocktitan.net and marketchameleon.com).
opportunities.rationale: Carnival reports Q1 FY2026 on Mar 27 BMO. Beat 4/4 last quarters (most recently +8.3% EPS surprise in Q3 2025). Record bookings through 2026, FY2025 adj. net income $3.1B record. CCL pulled back from $31.49 (Feb avg) to $24.57, down ~22% on broad market/Iran fuel cost fears, NOT company-specific impairment. WTI $98.23 is a fuel cost headwind but CCL has fuel hedging programs. Analysts estimate Q1 EPS $0.18 and revenue $6.14B. MODERATE: high-vol regime, fuel cost uncertainty, and 22% drawdown from highs create elevated entry risk. R:R from midpoint 24.50: stop 22.00 (-10.2%), target 29.00 (+18.4%), R:R 1:1.8. EXIT before Mar 27 BMO print. Invalidated below 22.00.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter on pullbacks toward 195-199 that hold above 185; target 225 (prior resistance zone and 200-day MA area).

Consolidated at 199.99 through Mar 20, holding the gap base. Enterprise IT spending bellwether signal is constructive. Regime headwind (high vol, VIX 26.78) caps conviction at MODERATE. R:R from midpoint 199 is 1:1.9 to 225 stop 185. Invalidated below 185 (pre-earnings swing low).
